title: cicuta maculata var. bolanderi
intro: Cicuta maculata var. bolanderi is a variety of the water hemlock plant, a highly toxic and invasive plant native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, it's essential to note that the plant's toxicity can cause severe health problems, and its use should be approached with extreme caution. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although the risk of poisoning makes its use generally discouraged.
